title: Private Letter — Ada Vieri
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_001_private_letter_ada_to_marta
SOURCE_TYPE: private_letter
AUTHOR: Ada Vieri
RECIPIENT: Marta Bellandi
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T09:10:00
LOCATION: Village Center
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.83

TEXT:
Marta,

Clara will bring the small parcel this afternoon: bread, honey, and the tincture Tommaso prepared. I told her to stay on the main road because Enrico mentioned fresh tracks near the birch crossing.

Please keep the latch closed until she arrives. She will knock three times.

Ada

title: Receipt — Pietro Lanza
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_002_bakery_receipt
SOURCE_TYPE: receipt
AUTHOR: Pietro Lanza
RECIPIENT: Ada Vieri
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T09:05:00
LOCATION: Lanza Bakery
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.82

TEXT:
Receipt: two small loaves, one honey jar, one brown cloth wrap, one sealed vial marked T.G. Paid in full by Ada Vieri. Parcel collected by Ada after first bell.

title: Private Note — Pietro Lanza
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_003_private_note_pietro_ledger
SOURCE_TYPE: private_note
AUTHOR: Pietro Lanza
RECIPIENT: Bakery Ledger
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T13:10:00
LOCATION: Lanza Bakery
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.76

TEXT:
Ada paid for two small loaves and asked that no one delay Clara. She seemed nervous about the north road and repeated Enrico's warning twice. I told her most people still use the main road when carrying parcels to the Old Mill.

title: Informal Note — Clara Vieri
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_004_informal_note_clara_to_livia
SOURCE_TYPE: informal_note
AUTHOR: Clara Vieri
RECIPIENT: Livia Ferri
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T13:42:00
LOCATION: Village Edge
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.74

TEXT:
Leaving now. Mother is being dramatic about the main road again. If I take Northwood I can be back before evening. Do not tell her unless I am late.

title: Ranger Log — Enrico Sarti
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_005_ranger_log_morning_tracks
SOURCE_TYPE: ranger_log
AUTHOR: Enrico Sarti
RECIPIENT: Municipal Archive
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T12:40:00
LOCATION: Northwood / Birch Crossing
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.79

TEXT:
Fresh tracks found near the birch crossing. Large canid. Direction uncertain, but at least one trail bends toward the Old Mill road. Advised Ada Vieri this morning not to let Clara take the shortcut.

title: Weather Bulletin — Weather Clerk
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_006_weather_bulletin_fog
SOURCE_TYPE: weather_bulletin
AUTHOR: Weather Clerk
RECIPIENT: Public Notice Board
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T06:00:00
LOCATION: Council House
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.87

TEXT:
Light fog expected after midday in the low parts of Northwood. Visibility near the birch crossing may be reduced between the second and third bells. Main road conditions remain clear.

title: Witness Statement — Pietro Lanza
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_007_witness_pietro_sees_clara
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Pietro Lanza
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T17:15:00
LOCATION: Guard Office
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.68

TEXT:
I saw Clara just before the second afternoon bell. She was at the north entrance, not on the main road. She had the parcel wrapped in brown cloth. I remember the scarf because it was the same color as the one Ada mended last week.

title: Informal Note — Clara Vieri
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_008_informal_note_clara_birches
SOURCE_TYPE: informal_note
AUTHOR: Clara Vieri
RECIPIENT: Livia Ferri
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T14:31:00
LOCATION: Northwood Path
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.75

TEXT:
Taking the shortcut after all. Met someone strange near the birches. He asked if I was going to the mill road. I probably should not have answered, but he seemed to already know Marta.

title: Witness Statement — Child Witness A
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_009_witness_child_at_birches
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Child Witness A
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T18:05:00
LOCATION: Village Square
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.36

TEXT:
I saw the girl with the dark red scarf near the white trees. She talked to something by the ditch. I thought it was a man in a brown coat, but it had a strange back. She pointed down the road toward the mill.

title: Witness Statement — Nino Falchi
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_010_witness_nino_dark_shape
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Nino Falchi
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T18:40:00
LOCATION: Guard Office
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.66

TEXT:
I saw a dark shape moving quickly toward the Old Mill road after three in the afternoon. I first thought it was a man carrying a cloak, but the movement was wrong. Low to the ground, then upright for a moment. I cannot swear it was the same creature Enrico has been tracking.

title: Witness Statement — Carlo Fenzi
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_011_old_mill_worker_noise
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Carlo Fenzi
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T18:55:00
LOCATION: Old Mill
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.64

TEXT:
At about three, I heard something strike the cottage door or wall. I did not look immediately because the mill wheel was making noise. A few minutes later the birds scattered from the hedge behind Marta's cottage.

title: Private Letter — Marta Bellandi
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_012_private_letter_marta_to_tommaso
SOURCE_TYPE: private_letter
AUTHOR: Marta Bellandi
RECIPIENT: Dr. Tommaso Grevi
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-12T18:30:00
LOCATION: Old Mill Cottage
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.80

TEXT:
Tommaso,

The draught you sent helped, but the noises behind the house have returned. Twice this week the rear latch was found lifted in the morning. I assumed it was the wind until I saw gray hair caught in the splintered wood.

Marta

title: Witness Statement — Sofia Rinaldi
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_013_witness_sofia_voice
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Sofia Rinaldi
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T17:20:00
LOCATION: Chapel Road
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.58

TEXT:
I was walking back from the chapel when I heard what sounded like Marta's voice from the direction of the Old Mill cottage. It must have been around a quarter to four, though I cannot be sure because the chapel bell had not rung correctly that day. The voice sounded strained, almost lower than usual. I did not go closer.

title: Parish Notice — Parish Office
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_014_chapel_bell_notice
SOURCE_TYPE: parish_notice
AUTHOR: Parish Office
RECIPIENT: Public Notice Board
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T07:30:00
LOCATION: Chapel Road
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.88

TEXT:
The chapel bell mechanism is under repair. Bells may ring late or unevenly until Master Bellini completes the replacement of the cracked wheel. Citizens should not rely on the chapel bell for precise timekeeping today.

title: Ranger Log — Enrico Sarti
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_015_ranger_log_afternoon_cry
SOURCE_TYPE: ranger_log
AUTHOR: Enrico Sarti
RECIPIENT: Municipal Archive
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T15:50:00
LOCATION: Old Mill Road
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.77

TEXT:
Heard a high cry from the direction of the Bellandi cottage. Found fresh scuffing on the path and broken fern stems. Proceeded toward the cottage without returning to the road station.

title: Official Report — Municipal Guard
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_016_guard_report_first_inspection
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Municipal Guard
RECIPIENT: Village Council
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T19:30:00
LOCATION: Guard Office
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.82

TEXT:
Initial inspection of the Bellandi cottage found signs of forced entry at the rear latch, disturbed bedding, broken ceramic near the hearth, and animal hair near the inner door. No money was missing. Food parcel was recovered unopened except for the honey jar.

title: Medical Report — Dr. Tommaso Grevi
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_017_medical_report_marta
SOURCE_TYPE: medical_report
AUTHOR: Dr. Tommaso Grevi
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T20:10:00
LOCATION: Bellandi Cottage
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.86

TEXT:
Marta Bellandi shows bruising around the shoulders and superficial tearing of the sleeve fabric. The pattern is consistent with a violent struggle, possibly involving an animal or a person wearing coarse hide. Estimated time of assault: between 15:00 and 15:40. Estimate uncertain due to room temperature and delayed examination.

title: Medical Report — Dr. Tommaso Grevi
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_018_medical_report_clara
SOURCE_TYPE: medical_report
AUTHOR: Dr. Tommaso Grevi
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-14T07:40:00
LOCATION: Village Clinic
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.88

TEXT:
Clara Vieri presents shock, minor scratches on left wrist, and wool fibers under fingernails. She reports grasping bedding while retreating from the figure in Marta's room. No evidence suggests she handled a blade or heavy tool.

title: Witness Statement — Clara Vieri
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_019_clara_statement_room
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Clara Vieri
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T19:05:00
LOCATION: Guard Office
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.72

TEXT:
When I reached Marta's room, the shutters were closed. The figure in the bed answered too slowly and the voice was rough, like someone speaking through cloth. I thought Marta was ill until I smelled wet animal hide. Then I saw the shape of the hands under the blanket and moved back.

title: Official Report — Municipal Guard
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_020_guard_report_second_inspection
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Municipal Guard
RECIPIENT: Village Council
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-14T09:25:00
LOCATION: Bellandi Cottage
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.84

TEXT:
Second inspection found fibers from Clara Vieri's parcel cloth near the bedside, suggesting the parcel was brought inside before the disturbance ended. No signs indicate theft as primary motive. The rear latch was damaged before the front door was opened.

title: Incident Note — Enrico Sarti
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_021_enrico_incident_note
SOURCE_TYPE: incident_note
AUTHOR: Enrico Sarti
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T18:15:00
LOCATION: Old Mill Cottage
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.74

TEXT:
Entered after hearing the girl cry out. Found Clara near the doorway and Marta on the floor beside the bed. The animal-like subject fled through the rear side after a struggle. I followed only to the hedge because Marta needed immediate help.

title: Administrative Record — Village Clerk
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_022_admin_record_enrico_ledger
SOURCE_TYPE: administrative_record
AUTHOR: Village Clerk
RECIPIENT: Municipal Archive
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T16:05:00
LOCATION: Council House
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.81

TEXT:
Enrico Sarti signed the supply ledger for two lanterns and one coil of rope. Note: entry copied into the ledger at 17:20 from a loose slip marked approximately 16:05. Exact signing time uncertain.

title: Witness Statement — Rinaldo Marchi
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_023_rinaldo_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Rinaldo Marchi
RECIPIENT: Municipal Guard
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T21:00:00
LOCATION: Guard Office
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.46

TEXT:
Yes, I argued with Marta two days ago. It was about payment, not violence. I left the village before noon and can prove I crossed the southern bridge while the afternoon market was being cleared. I never went near the Old Mill today.

title: Administrative Record — Bridge Toll Keeper
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_024_bridge_toll_rinaldo
SOURCE_TYPE: administrative_record
AUTHOR: Bridge Toll Keeper
RECIPIENT: Municipal Archive
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T14:55:00
LOCATION: Southern Bridge
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.74

TEXT:
Rinaldo Marchi crossed the southern bridge with two cloth bundles. Toll paid in copper. Entry time approximate; toll book line is smudged but appears after the 14:30 cart entry and before the 15:10 grain wagon entry.

title: Rumor Account — Lucia Berti
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_025_lucia_tavern_rumor
SOURCE_TYPE: rumor_account
AUTHOR: Lucia Berti
RECIPIENT: Valdombra Gazette
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T22:15:00
LOCATION: Lucia's Inn
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.41

TEXT:
People are too quick to blame beasts. Rinaldo was angry enough to split a table, and everyone heard it. If you ask me, the old debts matter more than footprints. The ranger likes tidy stories.

title: Local News — Valdombra Gazette
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_026_local_news_initial_rumors
SOURCE_TYPE: local_news
AUTHOR: Valdombra Gazette
RECIPIENT: Public
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-14T08:00:00
LOCATION: Valdombra
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.52

TEXT:
Rumors spread after yesterday's incident near the Old Mill. Some villagers claim a traveling merchant had recently argued with Marta Bellandi over unpaid remedies. Others insist that the old stories of the Northwood beast have returned. Officials have not confirmed either account.

title: Official Report — Municipal Guard
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_027_guard_report_rumor_origin
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Municipal Guard
RECIPIENT: Village Council
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-14T11:30:00
LOCATION: Guard Office
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.83

TEXT:
Interviews suggest the rumor about a debt dispute originated from Lucia Berti's inn before formal inspection results were available. No physical evidence currently connects Rinaldo Marchi to the Bellandi cottage. His bridge record is imperfect but broadly consistent with absence from the Old Mill road.

title: Market Notice — Market Office
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_028_market_notice_noise
SOURCE_TYPE: market_notice
AUTHOR: Market Office
RECIPIENT: Public
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T08:00:00
LOCATION: Village Square
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.90

TEXT:
The weekly chestnut market will move from the lower square to Chapel Road due to repairs near the fountain. Vendors of flour, wool, and dried apples should arrive before the second bell. Cart traffic may be heavier than usual.

title: School Notice — Agnese Conti
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_029_school_notice_noise
SOURCE_TYPE: school_notice
AUTHOR: Agnese Conti
RECIPIENT: Parents of Valdombra
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-13T11:00:00
LOCATION: Schoolhouse
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.84

TEXT:
Children will be dismissed early for harvest preparations. Please keep them away from Chapel Road carts and remind them not to play near the fountain repair works. The rehearsal costumes must be returned by evening.

title: Personal Recipe Card — Marta Bellandi
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_030_recipe_card_noise
SOURCE_TYPE: personal_recipe_card
AUTHOR: Marta Bellandi
RECIPIENT: Personal Papers
TIMESTAMP: 1842-10-10T12:00:00
LOCATION: Old Mill Cottage
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.72

TEXT:
Remedy for winter cough: honey, thyme, hot water, two drops bitter root tincture. Do not boil after adding tincture. Ada's girl dislikes the smell but it works well enough.
